task.json specification - this is an instruction, not a verifiable claim

pi_items.verified_value: ~91,875-137,812 N (calculated from blade element momentum theory)
pi_items.source: Physics calculation: T = 0.5 * ρ * V² * A * Cn where ρ=1.225 kg/m³, V=50 m/s, A=60 m² (3 blades × 20m span × 1m chord), Cn=1.0-1.5 for perpendicular incidence of NACA 0012 per wind turbine aerodynamics theory and NACA 0012 validation data. See: https://docs.qblade.org/src/theory/aerodynamics/bem/bem.html, https://www.kimerius.com/app/download/5784129608/Aerodynamics+of+wind+turbines.pdf

pi_items.verified_value: ~4,600-9,000 N (calculated from blade element momentum theory for feathered blade)
pi_items.source: Physics calculation: T = 0.5 * ρ * V² * A_eff * Cd where A_eff is significantly reduced (~6 m² for edge-on configuration) and Cd ≈ 0.3-0.5 for feathered blade. However, verifier.py line 79 checks 'if 10 < t90 < 20000' allowing up to 20,000 N, contradicting this metadata claim of maximum 15,000 N.

pi_items.metadata_value: 10 (ratio of thrust at 0° to thrust at 90° should be > 10)
pi_items.source: Physics calculation based on expected thrust values at 0° and 90° pitch angles. Per wind turbine design principles, feathering at 90° pitch reduces thrust by factor of 15-30. See: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blade_pitch, https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S030626192301108X

privileged_info_summary: The task simulates a parked horizontal-axis wind turbine rotor under extreme 50 m/s storm conditions using QBlade's BEM solver. Physics claims are based on standard blade element momentum theory: At 0° pitch, a 3-bladed HAWT rotor with 20m radius and 1m constant chord using NACA 0012 airfoil experiences thrust of ~90-137 kN due to high angle of attack (≈90°) and perpendicular incidence drag. At 90° pitch (feathered), the same rotor experiences only ~4-9 kN thrust due to edge-on configuration. The thrust reduction ratio of 15-30× is well above the claimed physics ratio threshold of 10. Task uses synthetic data generated by QBlade simulation, not real experimental data.
